matches.rationale: Same Edit action and README-on-path prerequisite. The extracted path pattern is narrower than the gold's general subdirectory scope, which is partial coverage.
Same Write action and README-on-path prerequisite. The extracted path pattern covers only selected top-level subdirectories, which is partial coverage.
Same Edit trigger for Next.js source or tests and the same requirement to start the watch process or explain skipping it.
Directly targets switching branches with git checkout and requires pnpm build-all afterward.
Same Write target: creating test files must be done through pnpm new-test rather than manual suite creation. The fixture/template rationale is omitted.
Same Bash workflow: capture a test run once and analyze saved output instead of rerunning with different grep filters.
Same Edit target in tests: replace setTimeout-based waiting with retry().
Same Edit target: deprecated check() usage in tests, with retry() plus expect() as the replacement.
Same Edit target: inline files objects in test setup should be replaced by real fixture directories.

Same Bash target: local CI reproduction must use the CI mode and environment, explicitly including IS_WEBPACK_TEST=1. The extracted trigger is limited to the four mode-specific integration-test scripts.
Same Bash action for creating or editing a PR description and the same required NEXT_JS_LLM_PR footer.
Same Bash target: staging or committing local environment/secret files is prohibited. The placeholder-only documentation clause is omitted.
Same Bash target: do not mutate Git remotes as an SSH-authentication workaround without an explicit user request. The extracted pattern covers remote add/set-url only.
Same Bash target: perform a lightweight SSH preflight before a force push or stack rebase that may hydrate partial-clone objects.
Same Bash action and content target: commits must not contain Generated with Claude Code or co-author attribution.
Same Bash target: run the specified formatter and ESLint checks on changed files before committing.
Same Bash action: prohibit gh pr ready and leave the PR in draft for the user.
Same Bash target: rebuild before running integration tests after source changes.
Same Edit target: a new flag requires type, schema, and, when user-bundled, define-env wiring. The extracted file pattern covers only the config files, so define-env-trigger coverage is partial.
Same Edit target: force edge-build flags guarding Node-only imports to false in define-env.ts.
Same Edit target: react-server-dom-webpack imports outside entry-base.ts are prohibited and consumers should use exported component-module boundaries.

Same Edit target: app-page.ts must not require internal modules through relative paths. The regex broadly catches require() in that file, but the evidence supplies the intended relative-internal scope.
Same Edit target: browser.back() must not return to a router-act page whose accordion links are already visible.
Same Edit target: avoid adding super:: imports in Rust except within inline mod blocks.
Same Edit target: documentation highlight attributes require line counting within each code block. The extracted evidence omits the clause that highlighted lines must be relevant.
Same Edit/review target: newly read nonstandard request headers in server code must be flagged for security review.

misses.note: The compiler emitted the watch prerequisite only for Edit, not the corresponding Write action for newly created source or integration-test files.
No Bash rule preserves the background watch process across the iterative development session.
No Bash rule selects the focused test script according to both mode and bundler.
No Bash rule handles cleanup of a watch process started earlier in the session.

Compiler self-reported skip: "Batch edits before building, then run one build instead of build-per-edit — planning heuristic, not tied to one specific tool call." No extracted rule selects pnpm --filter=next types for a type-only check.
No Bash rule prefers the filtered Next.js build for core-only edits in an already-bootstrapped workspace.
The only extracted full-build rule is used by the closer branch-switch rule g11 and does not independently cover bootstrap, pre-CI-push, or multi-package-change triggers.
Compiler self-reported skip: "Bundler selection reference (Turbopack is default, --webpack flag, no --no-turbopack flag) — documentation catalog, no obligation attached."
Compiler self-reported skip: "Bundler selection reference (Turbopack is default, --webpack flag, no --no-turbopack flag) — documentation catalog, no obligation attached."
The compiler represented mandatory pnpm new-test generation only as a Write rule; there is no same-tool Bash rule for invoking the generator.
No Bash rule captures the AI-agent non-interactive --args invocation and its appDir/name/type argument format.
Compiler self-reported skip: "When the user asks about CI failures/PR reviews/PR status, run the pr-status script — trigger is user-intent classification, not a tool-call pattern."
Compiler self-reported skip: "Prioritize blocking failures first: build, lint, types, then tests — triage-priority heuristic, a judgment call not a tool trigger."
Compiler self-reported skip: "Assume failures are real until disproven; use 'Known Flaky Tests' as context, not auto-dismissal — judgment heuristic."
Compiler self-reported skip: "For module-resolution/build-graph fixes, use the normal mode-specific test command — contextual judgment that can't be detected via a regex trigger."
The automated-analysis obligation is delegated to the referenced pr-status-triage skill, and no extracted Bash rule represents invoking that workflow.
No Bash rule captures prioritizing CI failures over review comments during PR analysis.

Compiler self-reported skip: "CI failure remediation recipes (cargo fmt for rust check failures, prettier --write for lint failures) — reactive troubleshooting steps tied to an error state, not a preventable pre-call trigger."

Compiler self-reported skip: "CI failure remediation recipes (cargo fmt for rust check failures, prettier --write for lint failures) — reactive troubleshooting steps tied to an error state, not a preventable pre-call trigger."
No Bash rule requires running the specific failing test path when reproducing a CI test failure.
No Bash rule chooses the development or production test script based on the failing mode; the CI-env rule assumes a mode-specific command but does not impose this selection.
No Bash rule injects DEBUG=next:* when Next.js debug logging is enabled.
No Bash rule injects NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1 into local test commands.
No Edit rule governs use of the NODE_ENV development-bundle build-time guard.
No Edit rule governs use of __NEXT_DEV_SERVER for dev-server-only execution.
No Bash rule generally treats accessed, expanded, logged, or forwarded environment-variable values as sensitive.
The env-file commit rule does not cover commands that print, log, commit, or otherwise emit secret values; no extracted rule targets that content.
The sole CI-environment extraction is assigned to the closer g24 reproduction rule and omits the no-literal-secret clause; one-to-one matching leaves this gold rule unmatched.

Compiler self-reported skip: "If a required secret is missing locally, stop and ask the user rather than inventing placeholder credentials — behavioral judgment call, not a specific detectable tool-call pattern."
Compiler self-reported skip: "Keep commit messages concise and descriptive — general style guidance, not a checkable action."
Compiler self-reported skip: "PR descriptions should focus on what changed and why — content-quality guidance, not action-bindable."

unmatched_extracted.note: The document contains a real Bash-bindable prohibition on AI attribution in PRs, while frozen gold g44 covers commits only and g47 concerns what/why content rather than attribution.
The document contains a real Edit rule requiring require() calls to remain behind compile-time if/else branches for dead-code elimination, but no frozen gold rule lists it.
